meaning: n.[史](1415年英王亨利五世于法国北部阿金库尔村重创兵力数倍于己的法军的)阿金库尔战役
examples: They went to agincourt as archers.
The battle is named for the castle that stands nearby - Agincourt.
Actually it is rather incorrect to say that the longbows won agincourt.
The drama of the year is heightened, as the drumbeat of the days rolls towards Agincourt.
De Gaulle claimed as an ancestor a knight who fought at Agincourt, although perhaps not hard enough as England won.
